codeReview.summary: The scaffold meets the feature's core requirements: it establishes a working Next.js + TypeScript baseline, adds the requested npm workflows and Jest coverage, and introduces a reusable BETTER-themed shell. I found two non-blocking follow-ups around mobile navigation behavior and layout-shell test coverage.

issues.description: On screens below the `sm` breakpoint, the primary nav links are hidden and replaced by `MobileMenuButton`, but that button has no open/close state or menu content. Mobile users therefore lose the header navigation shell the feature introduces.

The baseline tests only render `Home` and assert hero/placeholder text plus CTA hrefs. They do not exercise the shared `RootLayout`, so regressions in header navigation, footer framing, or the mobile-nav behavior above would go undetected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-experience-worker skill says to write failing tests first and to validate mobile/keyboard/fallback behavior for roadmap or visual surfaces, but this run implemented the shell before adding tests and recorded only desktop screenshot-based browser checks. The handoff still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true, so either adherence reporting or the skill guidance needs tightening.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md:22-28 requires test-first work plus mobile/keyboard/fallback browser validation. /Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/handoffs/2026-03-21T10-24-39-562Z__foundation-repo-scaffold-and-tooling__2eea01a8-9bc6-4847-8a4b-2cfc364cdbf3.json:36-40 records only a single navigate-and-screenshot browser check, and :66-69 marks `followedProcedure` true. /Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/worker-transcripts.jsonl:1 contains "Now let me create the baseline BETTER shell" before "Now let me add baseline tests".

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no shared reference for the project's preferred Next.js + Jest scaffold, and the worker spent a long segment repeatedly rewriting `jest.config.ts` and probing config keys before landing on `setupFilesAfterEnv`. A short library note or skill snippet for the approved Jest setup would likely save future workers time.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/worker-transcripts.jsonl:1 shows repeated `Create`/`Edit` cycles on `jest.config.ts`, multiple `npx jest --showConfig` / `require('jest-config')` checks, and several incorrect `setupFilesAfterSetup` attempts before the final `setupFilesAfterEnv` fix.

summary: Pass. The feature delivers the requested scaffold and themed shell, with two non-blocking follow-ups: the mobile header menu is inert on small screens, and the tests do not cover the shared layout shell.
